question.text: Under the SEBI (PIT) Regulations, 2015, when UPSI is shared for a transaction attracting open offer obligations, the board must be of opinion that:

answer.text: SEBI has granted prior approval for such sharing
The acquiring entity has executed a confidentiality agreement
The sharing of such information is in the best interests of the company
All shareholders have been notified of the proposed transaction

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 3(3)(i) permits sharing UPSI for transactions entailing open offer obligations where the board is of informed opinion that sharing is in the best interests of the company. **The Trap:** While confidentiality agreements are required (Regulation 3(4)), the board's primary obligation is to form an opinion on best interests. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX (Merchant Banking) covers UPSI sharing in M&A transactions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Under Regulation 3(4), parties must execute confidentiality and non-disclosure agreements as a separate requirement.
